description:
## Background

A first-time Beadsmith launch must no longer treat the application launch directory as a Workspace. The first complete user path is choosing a directory through the native picker, validating and normalizing it to a Beadwork Git root, loading all Issue Explorer views from that root, then durably making it Current. This establishes useful product behavior while retiring the process-cwd assumption.

## What to build

Deliver the first-time Current Workspace experience end to end. With no Current Workspace, show an empty chooser in the existing sidebar Current Workspace area. The user can invoke the native directory picker, choose a valid Beadwork repository, and see its complete Issue Explorer only after the backend has loaded and persisted it. Invalid choices leave the app unselected and show inline picker feedback.

## Technical Approach

- Build on the path-scoped workspace service. Register the Tauri dialog plugin and grant only the required picker permission; use asynchronous directory selection and treat cancellation as no operation.
- Replace the static sidebar workspace path with the compact inline selector direction: Current Workspace basename with full path as supporting text, plus a Choose folder action when no workspace is Current.
- On a valid candidate, add the validated root to the known catalog, load All/Ready/Blocked using the backend-held path, save current path and MRU state, then publish the complete Issue Explorer snapshot.
- On validation failure, retain no Current Workspace and render inline feedback. On first-load or store-save failure, retain the candidate as retryable when validation succeeded but do not claim it is Current.
- Remove `workspace.rs`, runtime cwd overrides, and `--workspace` application startup behavior as part of moving every production loader to the new path-scoped contract. Migrate baseline issue-list desktop tests to start empty and seed through the same typed workspace-switch operation the UI uses.

## Considerations

- The native dialog itself is not a reliable WebDriver target. Frontend integration tests cover picker wiring; desktop tests may invoke the typed switch operation that the picker calls.
- Do not briefly label a candidate as Current before its full Issue Explorer load and store save succeed.
- The picker can let the OS choose any directory; backend validation decides eligibility.
- Keep issue-list view/search behavior intact once a workspace has loaded.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A launch with no remembered Current Workspace renders an empty chooser, not a process-cwd error.
- [ ] Choosing a valid Beadwork repository through the selector loads All, Ready, and Blocked data and then presents it as Current.
- [ ] A non-Git or non-Beadwork directory produces inline validation feedback and changes neither Current Workspace nor Issue Explorer data.
- [ ] The persisted catalog/current state contains the validated repository root, not a selected subdirectory.
- [ ] Production startup and loaders no longer rely on `env::current_dir`, `env::set_current_dir`, or `--workspace`.
- [ ] The existing populated and true-empty Issue Explorer desktop scenarios run after being seeded through the typed switch path.

## Test Plan

- Rust and TauRPC tests for first successful selection, invalid selection, load failure, and store-save failure with no prior Current Workspace.
- React integration tests for the empty chooser, picker cancellation, valid selection state, and inline invalid-path feedback.
- Real desktop WebDriver coverage for empty launch followed by typed selection of populated and true-empty disposable Workspaces.
- Run `cargo test`, `pnpm test`, `pnpm check`, TypeScript checking, and the migrated Issue Explorer desktop suite.
title: Choose and load an initial Current Workspace
